title: Scrambled Eggs with Avocado and Salsa
description: Fluffy scrambled eggs topped with creamy avocado and fresh tomato salsa. A quick, protein-packed start to the day.
instructions: Mix diced tomatoes, cilantro, lime juice, and a pinch of salt to make a quick salsa. Set aside.
Whisk eggs with salt and pepper until well combined.
Heat ghee in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat. Pour in eggs and gently stir with a spatula, forming soft curds.
Remove from heat when eggs are just set but still slightly moist.
Plate eggs, top with sliced avocado and fresh salsa. Serve immediately.

title: Chicken Sausage Veggie Scramble
description: A hearty breakfast scramble with chicken sausage, peppers, onions, and eggs. One pan, big flavor.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced chicken sausage and cook until browned, about 4 minutes.
Add bell peppers and onion. Cook 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add spinach and stir until wilted, about 1 minute.
Whisk eggs with garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Pour over the vegetables and sausage.
Gently stir to form soft curds, cooking until eggs are just set, about 2-3 minutes.
Serve hot, optionally with sliced avocado on top.

title: Sweet Potato Toast with Fried Eggs
description: Thick slices of roasted sweet potato as toast, topped with fried eggs and everything bagel seasoning. A creative grain-free breakfast.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Brush sweet potato slices with 1 tbsp avocado oil and season with salt.
Bake on a sheet pan for 15-18 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender and lightly browned.
Fry eggs in remaining avocado oil over medium heat to desired doneness.
Spread mashed avocado on sweet potato toasts, top each with a fried egg.
Sprinkle with everything bagel seasoning and serve.

title: Beef Breakfast Burrito Bowl
description: All the flavors of a breakfast burrito in a bowl -- seasoned ground beef, eggs, avocado, and salsa over cauliflower rice.
instructions: Brown ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up. Season with cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, and half the salt. Drain and set aside.
In the same skillet, heat avocado oil and cook cauliflower rice for 3-4 minutes until tender. Season with remaining salt.
Scramble eggs in a separate pan over medium heat until just set.
Build bowls: cauliflower rice base, seasoned beef, scrambled eggs, diced avocado, and salsa.
Top with fresh cilantro and serve.

title: Smoked Salmon and Egg Stack
description: Poached eggs over smoked salmon with capers, red onion, and fresh dill. An elegant, nutrient-dense breakfast.
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a gentle simmer. Add white vinegar.
Create a gentle whirlpool and poach eggs one at a time for 3-4 minutes until whites are set.
Arrange smoked salmon on plates. Top with avocado slices and poached eggs.
Garnish with capers, red onion, and fresh dill. Drizzle with lemon juice.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

title: Turkey and Zucchini Breakfast Hash
description: Ground turkey sauteed with zucchini, mushrooms, and herbs. Light yet satisfying and great for meal prep.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground turkey and cook until browned, about 5 minutes, breaking it apart.
Add mushrooms and cook for 3 minutes until they release their liquid.
Add zucchini, garlic, thyme, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 5-6 minutes until zucchini is tender.
Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve hot, optionally topped with fried eggs.

title: Plantain and Egg Breakfast Skillet
description: Caramelized ripe plantains with eggs and crispy bacon. A Caribbean-inspired breakfast that is naturally grain-free.
instructions: Cook beef bacon in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy. Remove and set aside, keeping the drippings.
Add coconut oil to the skillet. Fry plantain slices for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and caramelized. Sprinkle with cinnamon.
Push plantains to the edges. Crack eggs into the center of the skillet.
Cover and cook 3-4 minutes until egg whites are set. Season with salt.
Crumble bacon over the top and serve directly from the skillet.

title: Berry Coconut Smoothie Bowl
description: A thick, creamy smoothie bowl with mixed berries and coconut milk, topped with seeds and fresh fruit.
instructions: Blend frozen berries, banana, coconut milk, and collagen peptides in a high-speed blender until thick and smooth. Add more coconut milk 1 tbsp at a time if needed -- keep it thick.
Pour into two bowls.
Top with hemp seeds, pumpkin seeds, coconut flakes, and fresh blueberries.
Serve immediately before it melts.

title: Chorizo and Sweet Potato Breakfast Bake
description: Spicy beef chorizo baked with sweet potatoes and eggs. A bold, satisfying one-dish breakfast for the whole family.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss sweet potatoes with avocado oil, cumin, and salt. Spread on a baking sheet and roast for 15 minutes.
While potatoes roast, brown chorizo in a skillet, breaking it apart. Add bell pepper and onion, cook 3-4 minutes.
Combine roasted sweet potatoes and chorizo mixture in a 9x13 baking dish.
Make 8 wells in the mixture. Crack an egg into each well.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until egg whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Garnish with fresh cilantro. Serve from the dish.

title: Coconut Flour Waffles
description: Light and fluffy waffles made with coconut flour. Naturally grain-free and perfect with fresh berries and maple syrup.
instructions: Preheat waffle iron and grease lightly with coconut oil.
Whisk eggs, coconut milk, melted coconut oil, honey, and vanilla in a bowl.
In a separate bowl, combine coconut flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add dry ingredients to wet and whisk until smooth. Let batter rest 3 minutes to thicken.
Pour batter into waffle iron (about 1/3 cup per waffle). Cook until golden and crisp, about 4-5 minutes per waffle.
Serve topped with fresh berries and a drizzle of honey.

title: Steak and Eggs with Herb Compound Ghee
description: Seared flank steak alongside sunny-side-up eggs, finished with herb-infused ghee. Classic high-protein fuel.
instructions: Season steak generously with salt and pepper. Let sit at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Heat a cast iron skillet over high heat. Add 1 tbsp ghee. Sear steak 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare. Rest 5 minutes, then slice against the grain.
Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining ghee, garlic, rosemary, and thyme. Cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Crack eggs into the herbed ghee. Cook sunny-side-up until whites are set, about 3 minutes.
Plate sliced steak alongside eggs. Spoon herbed ghee from the pan over everything.

title: Homemade Chicken Apple Sausage Patties
description: Savory-sweet breakfast sausage patties made from scratch with ground chicken, diced apple, and sage. Freezer-friendly.
instructions: Combine ground chicken, diced apple, sage, thyme, garlic powder, onion powder, nutmeg, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Mix gently with hands until just combined.
Form into 12 patties, about 2 inches across and 1/2 inch thick.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook patties in batches for 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through (165°F internal).
Serve hot. Store extras in the fridge up to 4 days or freeze up to 3 months.

title: Breakfast Egg-Stuffed Bell Peppers
description: Bell pepper halves filled with seasoned ground beef, topped with an egg, and baked to perfection. Colorful and nutritious.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Lightly oil a baking dish.
Brown ground beef in a skillet with garlic powder, smoked paprika, half the salt, and pepper. Drain excess fat.
Place pepper halves cut-side-up in the baking dish. Divide cooked beef among pepper halves.
Crack an egg into each pepper half. Season eggs with remaining salt.
Bake 20-22 minutes until egg whites are set.
Garnish with sliced green onions and serve.

title: Tropical Mango Chia Pudding
description: Overnight chia pudding with mango puree and coconut cream. A refreshing make-ahead tropical breakfast.
instructions: Blend mango until smooth to create a puree.
Whisk together chia seeds, coconut milk, mango puree, vanilla, and honey in a bowl.
Stir well, let sit 5 minutes, then stir again to prevent clumping.
Cover and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
Serve topped with sliced kiwi, coconut flakes, and chopped macadamia nuts.

title: Lamb Breakfast Meatballs with Mint
description: Savory ground lamb meatballs seasoned with cumin, coriander, and fresh mint. A Middle Eastern-inspired protein start.
instructions: Combine ground lamb, mint, parsley, garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, salt, and pepper. Mix gently until combined.
Roll into about 16 meatballs, roughly 1.5 inches each.
Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add meatballs and cook, turning occasionally, for 10-12 minutes until browned all over and cooked through.
Serve with sliced cucumbers, cherry tomatoes, and scrambled eggs for a complete breakfast.

title: Green Power Breakfast Smoothie
description: A nutrient-dense green smoothie with spinach, avocado, banana, and collagen protein. Thick enough to eat with a spoon.
instructions: Add coconut milk, spinach, and lemon juice to a high-speed blender. Blend until greens are fully broken down.
Add avocado, frozen banana, collagen, almond butter, and ginger. Blend until smooth and creamy.
Pour into glasses. Serve immediately.

title: BLT Egg Cups
description: Turkey bacon-wrapped egg cups with spinach and sun-dried tomatoes baked in a muffin tin. Grab-and-go perfection.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Line each muffin cup with a slice of turkey bacon around the edge.
Place a small amount of spinach and sun-dried tomatoes in each cup.
Crack an egg into each bacon-lined cup. Season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Bake for 15-18 minutes until egg whites are set.
Let cool 2 minutes in the tin before removing. Store in fridge up to 4 days.

title: Cassava Flour Breakfast Crepes
description: Thin, pliable crepes made with cassava flour and coconut milk. Fill with berries and almond butter for a special breakfast.
instructions: Whisk cassava flour, eggs, coconut milk, water, melted coconut oil, vanilla, and salt until smooth. Let batter rest 5 minutes.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Lightly grease with coconut oil.
Pour about 3 tbsp of batter, swirling the pan to create a thin even layer. Cook 1-2 minutes until edges lift, then flip and cook 30 seconds more.
Repeat with remaining batter, making about 8 crepes.
Fill each crepe with almond butter and sliced strawberries. Roll up and drizzle with honey.

title: Mediterranean Egg Scramble
description: Eggs scrambled with olives, artichoke hearts, sun-dried tomatoes, and fresh basil. A Mediterranean morning in a skillet.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Add artichoke hearts, sun-dried tomatoes, and olives. Warm through for 1-2 minutes.
Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes. Pour into the skillet.
Gently stir to form soft curds, about 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat while still slightly wet.
Top with torn fresh basil and serve.

title: Butternut Squash Breakfast Porridge
description: A warm, creamy porridge made from roasted butternut squash and coconut milk. A comforting grain-free alternative to oatmeal.
instructions: Combine mashed butternut squash and coconut milk in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add cinnamon, nutmeg, vanilla, honey, and salt. Stir until smooth and heated through, about 5-7 minutes.
Adjust consistency with more coconut milk if desired.
Divide into bowls and top with chopped pecans. Serve warm.

title: Spicy Ground Beef and Egg Skillet
description: Heavily seasoned ground beef with jalapeños, topped with fried eggs. A bold, fiery breakfast for those who like heat.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and jalapeños, cook 2 minutes.
Add ground beef, cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, and salt. Brown and break apart, about 5 minutes.
Make wells in the beef mixture and crack eggs into them.
Cover and cook 3-4 minutes until egg whites are set.
Garnish with cilantro and serve straight from the skillet.

title: Salmon and Avocado Breakfast Bowl
description: Flaked baked salmon over cauliflower rice with avocado, cucumber, and a sesame-lime dressing. A savory, omega-3-rich breakfast.
instructions: Season salmon with salt and pepper. Bake at 400°F for 12-14 minutes until flaky.
While salmon bakes, heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium heat. Cook cauliflower rice for 3-4 minutes. Season lightly with salt.
Whisk together coconut aminos, lime juice, and sesame oil for dressing.
Build bowls: cauliflower rice base, flaked salmon, sliced avocado, and cucumber.
Drizzle with dressing and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

title: Almond Flour Blueberry Muffins
description: Moist, tender muffins made with almond flour and fresh blueberries. Naturally sweetened and perfect for on-the-go mornings.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 12-cup muffin tin with parchment liners.
Whisk eggs, honey, melted coconut oil, and vanilla in a large bowl.
Add almond flour, baking soda, salt, and lemon zest. Stir until just combined.
Gently fold in blueberries.
Divide batter evenly among muffin cups, filling about 3/4 full.
Bake for 20-22 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean and tops are golden.
Cool in the tin 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. Store in an airtight container up to 4 days.

title: Carnitas Breakfast Plate
description: Slow-cooked beef carnitas served with eggs, plantains, and fresh pico de gallo. A Latin-inspired feast.
instructions: Make pico de gallo: combine diced tomatoes, onion, cilantro, lime juice, and a pinch of salt.
Heat 1 tbsp avocado oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Crisp shredded beef with cumin for 5-6 minutes until edges are browned.
In another skillet, fry plantain slices in 1 tbsp oil for 2-3 minutes per side until golden.
Fry eggs in remaining oil to desired doneness.
Plate: crispy carnitas, fried plantains, eggs, and a generous scoop of pico de gallo.

title: Mushroom and Spinach Omelette
description: A classic French-style omelette filled with sauteed mushrooms, spinach, and fresh herbs. Simple, elegant, and satisfying.
instructions: Heat 0.5 tbsp ghee in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Saute mushrooms 3-4 minutes until golden. Add spinach, stir until wilted. Remove and set aside.
Whisk eggs with salt and pepper. Heat remaining ghee in the same skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour in eggs. Tilt pan to spread evenly. As edges set, gently lift with a spatula to let uncooked egg flow underneath.
When mostly set but still slightly moist on top, spoon mushroom-spinach filling onto one half.
Fold omelette in half. Slide onto plate and garnish with chives.

title: Banana Collagen Protein Pancakes
description: Fluffy pancakes boosted with collagen peptides for extra protein. Made with cassava flour for a grain-free option.
instructions: Mash banana in a bowl. Add eggs, coconut milk, and collagen. Whisk until smooth.
Add cassava flour, baking powder, and cinnamon. Stir until just combined. Let rest 2 minutes.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium-low heat. Pour about 3 tbsp of batter per pancake.
Cook 2-3 minutes until bubbles form and edges set. Flip and cook 1-2 minutes more.
Serve stacked with fresh berries on top.

title: Turkey Bacon Sweet Potato Rounds
description: Roasted sweet potato rounds topped with turkey bacon, avocado, and a drizzle of hot sauce. Fun, stackable bites.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Brush sweet potato rounds with avocado oil and season with salt and pepper.
Bake on a sheet pan for 20-22 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender and golden.
Meanwhile, cook turkey bacon until crispy and crumble.
Top each sweet potato round with mashed avocado, crumbled turkey bacon, and a dash of hot sauce.
Serve as finger food or plate 4-5 rounds per person.

title: Chicken and Veggie Breakfast Stir-Fry
description: Leftover chicken stir-fried with broccoli, peppers, and coconut aminos. A savory Asian-inspired way to start the morning.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add broccoli and bell pepper, stir-fry 3-4 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and diced chicken. Cook 2 minutes until heated through.
Push everything to the sides. Crack eggs into the center and scramble until set.
Add coconut aminos and sesame oil. Toss everything together.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and serve hot.

title: Pumpkin Spice Noatmeal
description: Warm grain-free porridge made with ground flaxseed, pumpkin puree, and warming spices. All the cozy vibes without any grains.
instructions: Combine coconut milk and pumpkin puree in a small saucepan over medium heat. Stir until smooth.
Add ground flaxseed, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, cloves, and salt. Stir continuously for 3-4 minutes until thickened to porridge consistency.
Remove from heat. Stir in honey if using.
Divide into bowls and top with chopped pecans. Serve warm.

title: Zucchini Fritters with Eggs
description: Crispy pan-fried zucchini fritters made with almond flour, served alongside sunny eggs. A veggie-forward breakfast.
instructions: Grate zucchini and squeeze out as much moisture as possible using a clean towel.
Mix zucchini, 2 eggs, almond flour, green onions, garlic, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Heat avocado oil in a skillet over medium heat. Drop 2 tbsp of mixture per fritter and flatten slightly. Cook 3-4 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Fry remaining 2 eggs in the same skillet.
Serve fritters with fried eggs on top and a squeeze of lemon.

title: Acai Breakfast Bowl
description: A thick, antioxidant-rich acai base topped with banana, granola-free crunch, and drizzled with almond butter.
instructions: Break frozen acai packets into pieces. Add to a blender with frozen banana and coconut milk.
Blend until thick and smooth, scraping down sides as needed. The consistency should be thicker than a smoothie.
Divide between two bowls.
Top with sliced fresh banana, strawberries, coconut flakes, and hemp seeds.
Drizzle with almond butter and honey if using. Serve immediately.

title: Breakfast Taco Lettuce Wraps
description: Seasoned ground beef and scrambled eggs in crisp butter lettuce cups, topped with avocado and salsa. Low-carb taco morning.
instructions: Brown ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add taco seasoning and a splash of water. Simmer 2 minutes.
In a separate pan, heat avocado oil. Scramble eggs until just set.
Arrange butter lettuce cups on a platter.
Fill each cup with seasoned beef and scrambled eggs.
Top with diced avocado, salsa, and cilantro. Serve with lime wedges.

title: Sweet Potato Turkey Breakfast Casserole
description: A make-ahead casserole with layers of shredded sweet potato, ground turkey, and eggs. Cut into squares for easy weekday breakfasts.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9x13 baking dish with avocado oil.
Brown ground turkey with onion and garlic in a skillet. Add spinach and cook until wilted. Season with Italian seasoning, half the salt, and pepper.
Press shredded sweet potato into the bottom of the baking dish as a crust. Season with remaining salt. Bake 10 minutes to set.
Spread turkey mixture over the sweet potato layer.
Whisk eggs and pour evenly over everything.
Bake 30-35 minutes until eggs are set and top is lightly golden.
Let cool 10 minutes. Cut into 8 squares. Store in fridge up to 5 days.
